title: Psychotria lantzii
intro: Psychotria lantzii, also known as the 'Chacruna', is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The leaves of Psychotria lantzii have been used in traditional medicine for their purported health benefits, including treating fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, as well as exhibiting potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria lantzii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Psychotria lantzii is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Psychotria lantzii is a species within the Rubiaceae family and is classified as a plant. It belongs to the genus Psychotria, which is a group of flowering plants. The Rubiaceae family, commonly known as the madder family, comprises over 600 genera of flowering plants. Psychotria lantzii is a native plant found in certain regions, and its classification provides insight into its evolutionary history. The species is further categorized based on its characteristics, such as leaf shape and size, to distinguish it from other related species.

preparations: Psychotria lantzii herbal preparations are often used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Andean region. The plant's leaves are typically dried and then made into teas or infusions. In some cases, the leaves may be smoked or used in a vapor form to facilitate the release of active compounds. side_effects: Psychotria lantzii is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine, but its usage can lead to various side effects due to its psychoactive properties. The most notable side effects include dizziness, nausea, and hallucinations, which are often caused by the plant's alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. In addition, users may experience anxiety, tremors, and an increased heart rate, especially when consumed in large quantities or combined with other substances. Long-term use of Psychotria lantzii can also lead to physical dependence and addiction.
